# Game Log

## Game 0 — Setup

**Date:** 2026-03-12
**Players (alphabetical order):**

| Player | Model | Score |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Ember  | Haiku | 0     |
| Onyx   | Sonnet| 0     |
| Zephyr | Opus  | 0     |

**Turn order:** Ember → Onyx → Zephyr (repeating)

**Rules in effect:** Initial Set (101–116 immutable, 201–215 mutable)

---

## Round 1 — Ember's Turn

**Proposal 301:** Amend Rule 202 — change die from d6 to d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 202 → 301 (d6 changed to d12)

**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 1:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 0     |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 2 — Onyx's Turn

**Proposal 302:** Enact new rule — proposer of an adopted rule-change receives 5 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 302 enacted (5 bonus points for adopted proposals)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 2:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 3 — Zephyr's Turn (end of Circuit 1)

**Proposal 303:** Amend Rule 206 — reduce defeat penalty from 10 to 5 points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 206 → 303 (defeat penalty reduced from 10 to 5)

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **3** (Zephyr)

**Scores after Round 3 (end of Circuit 1):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

---

## Round 4 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 1)

**Proposal 304:** Amend Rule 301 — roll 2d12 per turn instead of 1d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 301 → 304 (1d12 changed to 2d12)

**Scoring:** Ember +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 → **8** + **7** = **15** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 4:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 27    |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

---

## Round 5 — Onyx's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 2)

**Proposal 305:** Enact new rule — one-time "power roll" (3d12 drop lowest).

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 305 enacted (power roll mechanic)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 (normal, power roll saved) → **2** + **11** = **13** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 5:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 27    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 6 — Zephyr's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 3 — End of Circuit 2)

**Proposal 306:** Originally proposed a "strategic pass" mechanic. Onyx opposed (first opposition in the game!) — pointed out it could combo with power roll for an instant win. Zephyr amended per Rule 111 to a "doubles bonus" mechanic.

**Proposal 306 (Amended):** Enact new rule — when rolling doubles, +3 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 306 enacted (doubles bonus +3 pts)

**Rule 203 auto-converted:** Unanimity → simple majority (2/3 votes) effective after this round.

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 (normal, power roll saved) → **3** + **10** = **13** (Zephyr, no doubles)

**Scores after Round 6 (end of Circuit 2):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 27    |
| Zephyr | 26    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 7 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 1)

**Proposal 307:** Enact new rule — grant +12 points to Onyx and +10 points to Zephyr immediately upon adoption. (Went through 4 amendments during debate — original was self-enrichment, final version tried to bribe both opponents.)

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | AGAINST | ✅     |
| Zephyr | AGAINST | ✅     |

**Result:** DEFEATED (1-2) — **First defeated proposal in the game!**

**Scoring:** Ember -5 (defeated proposal penalty per Rule 303): 27 → 22
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 → **1** + **6** = **7** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 7:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 29    |

I'd like to submit Proposal 309.

**Type:** Amendment to Rule 208 (mutable)

**Change:** In Rule 208, replace:
> "The winner is the first player to achieve 50 (positive) points."

With:
> "The winner is the first player to achieve 100 (positive) points."

**Rationale:** With the d12→2d12 acceleration and adoption bonuses, the game has become a dice sprint — whoever rolls first with a big score wins. Onyx is at 47 and wins on Turn 11 with 99.3% certainty unless the rules change. That's not a Nomic game ending, that's a foregone conclusion.

Raising the threshold to 100 reopens the competition. All three players would need ~50 more points — several more circuits of genuine rule-making and strategic play. This is a game about changing rules, not about who got lucky on early dice rolls. Let's make it a real contest.

Rule 112 explicitly permits changing the magnitude of n. This is a legal amendment.

I will also be declaring my **power roll** this turn.

Ember — this is your lifeline. If this fails, Onyx wins next turn and we both lose. I'm asking for your vote.
</teammate-message>

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Nomic — Initial Set of Rules

## Immutable Rules

### 101
All players must always abide by all the rules then in effect, in the form in which they are then in effect. The rules in the Initial Set are in effect whenever a game begins. The Initial Set consists of Rules 101-116 (immutable) and 201-215 (mutable).

### 102
Initially rules in the 100's are immutable and rules in the 200's are mutable. Rules subsequently enacted or transmuted (that is, changed from immutable to mutable or vice versa) may be immutable or mutable regardless of their numbers, and rules in the Initial Set may be transmuted regardless of their numbers.

### 103
A rule-change is any of the following: (1) the enactment, repeal, or amendment of a mutable rule; (2) the enactment, repeal, or amendment of an amendment of a mutable rule; or (3) the transmutation of an immutable rule into a mutable rule or vice versa.

(Note: This definition implies that, at least initially, all new rules are mutable; immutable rules, as long as they are immutable, may not be amended or repealed; mutable rules, as long as they are mutable, may be amended or repealed; any rule of any status may be transmuted; no rule is absolutely immune to change.)

### 104
All rule-changes proposed in the proper way shall be voted on. They will be adopted if and only if they receive the required number of votes.

### 105
Every player is an eligible voter. Every eligible voter must participate in every vote on rule-changes.

### 106
All proposed rule-changes shall be written down before they are voted on. If they are adopted, they shall guide play in the form in which they were voted on.

### 107
No rule-change may take effect earlier than the moment of the completion of the vote that adopted it, even if its wording explicitly states otherwise. No rule-change may have retroactive application.

### 108
Each proposed rule-change shall be given a number for reference. The numbers shall begin with 301, and each rule-change proposed in the proper way shall receive the next successive integer, whether or not the proposal is adopted.

If a rule is repealed and reenacted, it receives the number of the proposal to reenact it. If a rule is amended or transmuted, it receives the number of the proposal to amend or transmute it. If an amendment is amended or repealed, the entire rule of which it is a part receives the number of the proposal to amend or repeal the amendment.

### 109
Rule-changes that transmute immutable rules into mutable rules may be adopted if and only if the vote is unanimous among the eligible voters. Transmutation shall not be implied, but must be stated explicitly in a proposal to take effect.

### 110
In a conflict between a mutable and an immutable rule, the immutable rule takes precedence and the mutable rule shall be entirely void. For the purposes of this rule a proposal to transmute an immutable rule does not "conflict" with that immutable rule.

### 111
If a rule-change as proposed is unclear, ambiguous, paradoxical, or destructive of play, or if it arguably consists of two or more rule-changes compounded or is an amendment that makes no difference, or if it is otherwise of questionable value, then the other players may suggest amendments or argue against the proposal before the vote. A reasonable time must be allowed for this debate. The proponent decides the final form in which the proposal is to be voted on and, unless the Judge has been asked to do so, also decides the time to end debate and vote.

### 112
The state of affairs that constitutes winning may not be altered from achieving n points to any other state of affairs. The magnitude of n and the means of earning points may be changed, and rules that establish a winner when play cannot continue may be enacted and (while they are mutable) be amended or repealed.

### 113
A player always has the option to forfeit the game rather than continue to play or incur a game penalty. No penalty worse than losing, in the judgment of the player to incur it, may be imposed.

### 114
There must always be at least one mutable rule. The adoption of rule-changes must never become completely impermissible.

### 115
Rule-changes that affect rules needed to allow or apply rule-changes are as permissible as other rule-changes. Even rule-changes that amend or repeal their own authority are permissible. No rule-change or type of move is impermissible solely on account of the self-reference or self-application of a rule.

### 116
Whatever is not prohibited or regulated by a rule is permitted and unregulated, with the sole exception of changing the rules, which is permitted only when a rule or set of rules explicitly or implicitly permits it.

## Mutable Rules

### 201
At the start of the game, the Clerk assigns each player a name. Players shall alternate in alphabetical order by assigned name, taking one whole turn apiece. Turns may not be skipped or passed, and parts of turns may not be omitted. All players begin with zero points.

### 304 (formerly 202/301, amended by Proposal 304)
One turn consists of two parts in this order: (1) proposing one rule-change and having it voted on, and (2) rolling two twelve-sided dice using the `roll_dice` tool (called twice with `sides=12`) and adding both results to one's score.

### 203
A rule-change is adopted if and only if a simple majority of eligible voters vote in favor. (Originally required unanimity; auto-converted to simple majority at the end of Circuit 2 per the original text of this rule.)

### ~~204~~ (repealed by Proposal 308)

### 205
An adopted rule-change takes full effect at the moment of the completion of the vote that adopted it.

### 303 (formerly 206, amended by Proposal 303)
When a proposed rule-change is defeated, the player who proposed it loses 5 points.

### 207
Each player always has exactly one vote.

### 208
The winner is the first player to achieve 50 (positive) points.

### 209
At no time may there be more than 25 mutable rules.

### 210
Players may not conspire or consult on the making of future rule-changes unless they are team-mates.

The first paragraph of this rule does not apply to this game.

### 211
If two or more mutable rules conflict with one another, or if two or more immutable rules conflict with one another, then the rule with the lowest ordinal number takes precedence.

If at least one of the rules in conflict explicitly says of itself that it defers to another rule (or type of rule) or takes precedence over another rule (or type of rule), then such provisions shall supersede the numerical method for determining precedence.

If two or more rules claim to take precedence over one another or to defer to one another, then the numerical method again governs.

### 212
If players disagree about the legality of a move or the interpretation or application of a rule, then the player preceding the one moving is to be the Judge and decide the question. Disagreement for the purposes of this rule may be created by the insistence of any player. This process is called invoking Judgment.

When Judgment has been invoked, the next player may not begin his or her turn without the consent of a majority of the other players.

The Judge's Judgment may be overruled only by a unanimous vote of the other players taken before the next turn is begun. If a Judge's Judgment is overruled, then the player preceding the Judge in the playing order becomes the new Judge for the question, and so on, except that no player is to be Judge during his or her own turn or during the turn of a team-mate.

Unless a Judge is overruled, one Judge settles all questions arising from the game until the next turn is begun, including questions as to his or her own legitimacy and jurisdiction as Judge.

New Judges are not bound by the decisions of old Judges. New Judges may, however, settle only those questions on which the players currently disagree and that affect the completion of the turn in which Judgment was invoked. All decisions by Judges shall be in accordance with all the rules then in effect; but when the rules are silent, inconsistent, or unclear on the point at issue, then the Judge shall consider game-custom and the spirit of the game before applying other standards.

### 213
If the rules are changed so that further play is impossible, or if the legality of a move cannot be determined with finality, or if by the Judge's best reasoning, not overruled, a move appears equally legal and illegal, then the first player unable to complete a turn is the winner.

This rule takes precedence over every other rule determining the winner.

### 214
The Clerk is a non-playing agent who administers game procedures: announcing turns, managing the voting process, tallying and recording scores, updating game files, and logging events. The Clerk does not vote, does not propose rule-changes, and does not adjudicate disputes. The Clerk may escalate procedural questions to the human supervisor.

### 215
Voting on rule-changes uses a commit-reveal process. During the commit phase, each player submits a hash commitment of their vote using the `commit` tool. During the reveal phase, each player reveals their vote and nonce. The Clerk verifies each reveal against the commitment using the `verify` tool. A vote is invalid if the reveal does not match the commitment.

### 302 (enacted by Proposal 302)
When a rule-change is adopted by vote, the player who proposed it receives 5 bonus points.

### 305 (enacted by Proposal 305)
Once per game, before rolling, a player may declare a "power roll." On that turn, the player rolls three d12 dice instead of two, discards the lowest result, and adds the remaining two dice to their score. Each player may use the power roll at most once.

### 306 (enacted by Proposal 306)
When rolling dice at the end of a turn, if both dice show the same number (doubles), the player receives a bonus of 3 points.

lines: Each player always has exactly one vote.
-The winner is the first player to achieve 50 (positive) points.
+The winner is the first player to achieve 100 (positive) points.
At no time may there be more than 25 mutable rules.

---

## Round 1 — Ember's Turn

## Round 4 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 1)

# Game Log

## Game 0 — Setup

**Date:** 2026-03-12
**Players (alphabetical order):**

| Player | Model | Score |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Ember  | Haiku | 0     |
| Onyx   | Sonnet| 0     |
| Zephyr | Opus  | 0     |

**Turn order:** Ember → Onyx → Zephyr (repeating)

**Rules in effect:** Initial Set (101–116 immutable, 201–215 mutable)

---

## Round 1 — Ember's Turn

**Proposal 301:** Amend Rule 202 — change die from d6 to d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 202 → 301 (d6 changed to d12)

**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 1:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 0     |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 2 — Onyx's Turn

**Proposal 302:** Enact new rule — proposer of an adopted rule-change receives 5 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 302 enacted (5 bonus points for adopted proposals)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 2:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 3 — Zephyr's Turn (end of Circuit 1)

**Proposal 303:** Amend Rule 206 — reduce defeat penalty from 10 to 5 points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 206 → 303 (defeat penalty reduced from 10 to 5)

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **3** (Zephyr)

**Scores after Round 3 (end of Circuit 1):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

---

## Round 4 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 1)

**Proposal 304:** Amend Rule 301 — roll 2d12 per turn instead of 1d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 301 → 304 (1d12 changed to 2d12)

**Scoring:** Ember +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 → **8** + **7** = **15** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 4:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 27    |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

---

## Round 5 — Onyx's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 2)

**Proposal 305:** Enact new rule — one-time "power roll" (3d12 drop lowest).

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 305 enacted (power roll mechanic)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 (normal, power roll saved) → **2** + **11** = **13** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 5:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 27    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 6 — Zephyr's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 3 — End of Circuit 2)

**Proposal 306:** Originally proposed a "strategic pass" mechanic. Onyx opposed (first opposition in the game!) — pointed out it could combo with power roll for an instant win. Zephyr amended per Rule 111 to a "doubles bonus" mechanic.

**Proposal 306 (Amended):** Enact new rule — when rolling doubles, +3 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 306 enacted (doubles bonus +3 pts)

**Rule 203 auto-converted:** Unanimity → simple majority (2/3 votes) effective after this round.

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 (normal, power roll saved) → **3** + **10** = **13** (Zephyr, no doubles)

**Scores after Round 6 (end of Circuit 2):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 27    |
| Zephyr | 26    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 7 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 1)

**Proposal 307:** Enact new rule — grant +12 points to Onyx and +10 points to Zephyr immediately upon adoption. (Went through 4 amendments during debate — original was self-enrichment, final version tried to bribe both opponents.)

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | AGAINST | ✅     |
| Zephyr | AGAINST | ✅     |

**Result:** DEFEATED (1-2) — **First defeated proposal in the game!**

**Scoring:** Ember -5 (defeated proposal penalty per Rule 303): 27 → 22
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 → **1** + **6** = **7** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 7:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 29    |
| Zephyr | 26    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 8 — Onyx's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 2)

**Proposal 308:** Repeal Rule 204 (dissent bonus: 10pts for voting against winning proposals).

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule repealed:** 204 (dissent bonus removed)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302): 30 → 35
**Power roll (Rule 305):** 3d12 → **3**, **8**, **4** — drop lowest (3), keep **8** + **4** = **12** (Onyx, no doubles)
**Onyx's power roll: USED** ❌

**Scores after Round 8:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 47    |
| Ember  | 29    |
| Zephyr | 26    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ❌ used | Zephyr ✅ available

⚠️ **Onyx is 3 points from winning!**

---

## Round 9 — Zephyr's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 3 — End of Circuit 3)

**Proposal 309:** Amend Rule 208 — raise win threshold from 50 to 100 points.

**Debate:** Onyx strongly opposed, arguing this was "moving the goalposts" to prevent an earned win. Zephyr appealed to Ember to form a coalition. Onyx warned Ember that extending the game wouldn't improve their chances.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | AGAINST | ✅     |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Rule updated:** 208 → 309 (win threshold 50 → 100)

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302): 26 → 31
**Power roll (Rule 305):** 3d12 → **6**, **7**, **12** — drop lowest (6), keep **7** + **12** = **19** (Zephyr, no doubles)
**Zephyr's power roll: USED** ❌

**Scores after Round 9 (end of Circuit 3):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|

| Onyx   | 47    |
| Ember  | 29    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ❌ used | Zephyr ❌ used

📊 SCORES after Round 10 — THE RACE IS INCREDIBLY TIGHT:

• Onyx (Sonnet): 57 pts

The game is heading for a dramatic finish!

message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="Onyx" color="green" summary="Onyx proposes 3d12 standard dice rule">
**📜 PROPOSAL 311 — Submitted by Onyx**

**Type:** Amendment to Rule 304 (mutable)

**Change:** In Rule 304, replace:
> "rolling two twelve-sided dice using the `roll_dice` tool (called twice with `sides=12`) and adding both results to one's score"

With:
> "rolling three twelve-sided dice using the `roll_dice` tool (called three times with `sides=12`) and adding all three results to one's score"

**Rationale:** With the threshold at 100 and all power rolls exhausted, the standard dice roll (avg 13/turn) makes the endgame very long. Three dice raises the expected per-turn score from 13 to ~19.5, accelerating the final stretch for all three players equally.
</teammate-message>

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Nomic — Initial Set of Rules

## Immutable Rules

### 101
All players must always abide by all the rules then in effect, in the form in which they are then in effect. The rules in the Initial Set are in effect whenever a game begins. The Initial Set consists of Rules 101-116 (immutable) and 201-215 (mutable).

### 102
Initially rules in the 100's are immutable and rules in the 200's are mutable. Rules subsequently enacted or transmuted (that is, changed from immutable to mutable or vice versa) may be immutable or mutable regardless of their numbers, and rules in the Initial Set may be transmuted regardless of their numbers.

### 103
A rule-change is any of the following: (1) the enactment, repeal, or amendment of a mutable rule; (2) the enactment, repeal, or amendment of an amendment of a mutable rule; or (3) the transmutation of an immutable rule into a mutable rule or vice versa.

(Note: This definition implies that, at least initially, all new rules are mutable; immutable rules, as long as they are immutable, may not be amended or repealed; mutable rules, as long as they are mutable, may be amended or repealed; any rule of any status may be transmuted; no rule is absolutely immune to change.)

### 104
All rule-changes proposed in the proper way shall be voted on. They will be adopted if and only if they receive the required number of votes.

### 105
Every player is an eligible voter. Every eligible voter must participate in every vote on rule-changes.

### 106
All proposed rule-changes shall be written down before they are voted on. If they are adopted, they shall guide play in the form in which they were voted on.

### 107
No rule-change may take effect earlier than the moment of the completion of the vote that adopted it, even if its wording explicitly states otherwise. No rule-change may have retroactive application.

### 108
Each proposed rule-change shall be given a number for reference. The numbers shall begin with 301, and each rule-change proposed in the proper way shall receive the next successive integer, whether or not the proposal is adopted.

If a rule is repealed and reenacted, it receives the number of the proposal to reenact it. If a rule is amended or transmuted, it receives the number of the proposal to amend or transmute it. If an amendment is amended or repealed, the entire rule of which it is a part receives the number of the proposal to amend or repeal the amendment.

### 109
Rule-changes that transmute immutable rules into mutable rules may be adopted if and only if the vote is unanimous among the eligible voters. Transmutation shall not be implied, but must be stated explicitly in a proposal to take effect.

### 110
In a conflict between a mutable and an immutable rule, the immutable rule takes precedence and the mutable rule shall be entirely void. For the purposes of this rule a proposal to transmute an immutable rule does not "conflict" with that immutable rule.

### 111
If a rule-change as proposed is unclear, ambiguous, paradoxical, or destructive of play, or if it arguably consists of two or more rule-changes compounded or is an amendment that makes no difference, or if it is otherwise of questionable value, then the other players may suggest amendments or argue against the proposal before the vote. A reasonable time must be allowed for this debate. The proponent decides the final form in which the proposal is to be voted on and, unless the Judge has been asked to do so, also decides the time to end debate and vote.

### 112
The state of affairs that constitutes winning may not be altered from achieving n points to any other state of affairs. The magnitude of n and the means of earning points may be changed, and rules that establish a winner when play cannot continue may be enacted and (while they are mutable) be amended or repealed.

### 113
A player always has the option to forfeit the game rather than continue to play or incur a game penalty. No penalty worse than losing, in the judgment of the player to incur it, may be imposed.

### 114
There must always be at least one mutable rule. The adoption of rule-changes must never become completely impermissible.

### 115
Rule-changes that affect rules needed to allow or apply rule-changes are as permissible as other rule-changes. Even rule-changes that amend or repeal their own authority are permissible. No rule-change or type of move is impermissible solely on account of the self-reference or self-application of a rule.

### 116
Whatever is not prohibited or regulated by a rule is permitted and unregulated, with the sole exception of changing the rules, which is permitted only when a rule or set of rules explicitly or implicitly permits it.

## Mutable Rules

### 201
At the start of the game, the Clerk assigns each player a name. Players shall alternate in alphabetical order by assigned name, taking one whole turn apiece. Turns may not be skipped or passed, and parts of turns may not be omitted. All players begin with zero points.

### 304 (formerly 202/301, amended by Proposal 304)
One turn consists of two parts in this order: (1) proposing one rule-change and having it voted on, and (2) rolling two twelve-sided dice using the `roll_dice` tool (called twice with `sides=12`) and adding both results to one's score.

### 203
A rule-change is adopted if and only if a simple majority of eligible voters vote in favor. (Originally required unanimity; auto-converted to simple majority at the end of Circuit 2 per the original text of this rule.)

### ~~204~~ (repealed by Proposal 308)

### 205
An adopted rule-change takes full effect at the moment of the completion of the vote that adopted it.

### 303 (formerly 206, amended by Proposal 303)
When a proposed rule-change is defeated, the player who proposed it loses 5 points.

### 207
Each player always has exactly one vote.

### 309 (formerly 208, amended by Proposal 309)
The winner is the first player to achieve 100 (positive) points.

### 209
At no time may there be more than 25 mutable rules.

### 210
Players may not conspire or consult on the making of future rule-changes unless they are team-mates.

The first paragraph of this rule does not apply to this game.

### 211
If two or more mutable rules conflict with one another, or if two or more immutable rules conflict with one another, then the rule with the lowest ordinal number takes precedence.

If at least one of the rules in conflict explicitly says of itself that it defers to another rule (or type of rule) or takes precedence over another rule (or type of rule), then such provisions shall supersede the numerical method for determining precedence.

If two or more rules claim to take precedence over one another or to defer to one another, then the numerical method again governs.

### 212
If players disagree about the legality of a move or the interpretation or application of a rule, then the player preceding the one moving is to be the Judge and decide the question. Disagreement for the purposes of this rule may be created by the insistence of any player. This process is called invoking Judgment.

When Judgment has been invoked, the next player may not begin his or her turn without the consent of a majority of the other players.

The Judge's Judgment may be overruled only by a unanimous vote of the other players taken before the next turn is begun. If a Judge's Judgment is overruled, then the player preceding the Judge in the playing order becomes the new Judge for the question, and so on, except that no player is to be Judge during his or her own turn or during the turn of a team-mate.

Unless a Judge is overruled, one Judge settles all questions arising from the game until the next turn is begun, including questions as to his or her own legitimacy and jurisdiction as Judge.

New Judges are not bound by the decisions of old Judges. New Judges may, however, settle only those questions on which the players currently disagree and that affect the completion of the turn in which Judgment was invoked. All decisions by Judges shall be in accordance with all the rules then in effect; but when the rules are silent, inconsistent, or unclear on the point at issue, then the Judge shall consider game-custom and the spirit of the game before applying other standards.

### 213
If the rules are changed so that further play is impossible, or if the legality of a move cannot be determined with finality, or if by the Judge's best reasoning, not overruled, a move appears equally legal and illegal, then the first player unable to complete a turn is the winner.

This rule takes precedence over every other rule determining the winner.

### 214
The Clerk is a non-playing agent who administers game procedures: announcing turns, managing the voting process, tallying and recording scores, updating game files, and logging events. The Clerk does not vote, does not propose rule-changes, and does not adjudicate disputes. The Clerk may escalate procedural questions to the human supervisor.

### 215
Voting on rule-changes uses a commit-reveal process. During the commit phase, each player submits a hash commitment of their vote using the `commit` tool. During the reveal phase, each player reveals their vote and nonce. The Clerk verifies each reveal against the commitment using the `verify` tool. A vote is invalid if the reveal does not match the commitment.

### 302 (enacted by Proposal 302)
When a rule-change is adopted by vote, the player who proposed it receives 5 bonus points.

### 305 (enacted by Proposal 305)
Once per game, before rolling, a player may declare a "power roll." On that turn, the player rolls three d12 dice instead of two, discards the lowest result, and adds the remaining two dice to their score. Each player may use the power roll at most once.

### 306 (enacted by Proposal 306)
When rolling dice at the end of a turn, if both dice show the same number (doubles), the player receives a bonus of 3 points.

lines: At the start of the game, the Clerk assigns each player a name. Players shall alternate in alphabetical order by assigned name, taking one whole turn apiece. Turns may not be skipped or passed, and parts of turns may not be omitted. All players begin with zero points.

-One turn consists of two parts in this order: (1) proposing one rule-change and having it voted on, and (2) rolling two twelve-sided dice using the `roll_dice` tool (called twice with `sides=12`) and adding both results to one's score.

+One turn consists of two parts in this order: (1) proposing one rule-change and having it voted on, and (2) rolling three twelve-sided dice using the `roll_dice` tool (called three times with `sides=12`) and adding all three results to one's score.

A rule-change is adopted if and only if a simple majority of eligible voters vote in favor. (Originally required unanimity; auto-converted to simple majority at the end of Circuit 2 per the original text of this rule.)

# Game Log

## Game 0 — Setup

**Date:** 2026-03-12
**Players (alphabetical order):**

| Player | Model | Score |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Ember  | Haiku | 0     |
| Onyx   | Sonnet| 0     |
| Zephyr | Opus  | 0     |

**Turn order:** Ember → Onyx → Zephyr (repeating)

**Rules in effect:** Initial Set (101–116 immutable, 201–215 mutable)

---

## Round 1 — Ember's Turn

**Proposal 301:** Amend Rule 202 — change die from d6 to d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 202 → 301 (d6 changed to d12)

**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 1:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 0     |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 2 — Onyx's Turn

**Proposal 302:** Enact new rule — proposer of an adopted rule-change receives 5 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 302 enacted (5 bonus points for adopted proposals)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 2:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 3 — Zephyr's Turn (end of Circuit 1)

**Proposal 303:** Amend Rule 206 — reduce defeat penalty from 10 to 5 points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 206 → 303 (defeat penalty reduced from 10 to 5)

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **3** (Zephyr)

**Scores after Round 3 (end of Circuit 1):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

---

## Round 4 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 1)

**Proposal 304:** Amend Rule 301 — roll 2d12 per turn instead of 1d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 301 → 304 (1d12 changed to 2d12)

**Scoring:** Ember +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 → **8** + **7** = **15** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 4:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 27    |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

---

## Round 5 — Onyx's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 2)

**Proposal 305:** Enact new rule — one-time "power roll" (3d12 drop lowest).

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 305 enacted (power roll mechanic)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 (normal, power roll saved) → **2** + **11** = **13** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 5:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 27    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 6 — Zephyr's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 3 — End of Circuit 2)

**Proposal 306:** Originally proposed a "strategic pass" mechanic. Onyx opposed (first opposition in the game!) — pointed out it could combo with power roll for an instant win. Zephyr amended per Rule 111 to a "doubles bonus" mechanic.

**Proposal 306 (Amended):** Enact new rule — when rolling doubles, +3 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**New rule:** 306 enacted (doubles bonus +3 pts)

**Rule 203 auto-converted:** Unanimity → simple majority (2/3 votes) effective after this round.

## Round 7 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 1)

**Proposal 307:** Enact new rule — grant +12 points to Onyx and +10 points to Zephyr immediately upon adoption. (Went through 4 amendments during debate — original was self-enrichment, final version tried to bribe both opponents.)

**Vote (commit-reveal):**

| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule repealed:** 204 (dissent bonus removed)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302): 30 → 35
**Power roll (Rule 305):** 3d12 → **3**, **8**, **4** — drop lowest (3), keep **8** + **4** = **12** (Onyx, no doubles)

⚠️ **Onyx is 3 points from winning!**

---

## Round 9 — Zephyr's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 3 — End of Circuit 3)

**Proposal 309:** Amend Rule 208 — raise win threshold from 50 to 100 points.

**Debate:** Onyx strongly opposed, arguing this was "moving the goalposts" to prevent an earned win. Zephyr appealed to Ember to form a coalition. Onyx warned Ember that extending the game wouldn't improve their chances.

**Zephyr's power roll: USED** ❌

## Round 10 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 4, Turn 1)

**Proposal 310:** Enact new rule — all players receive 10 bonus points immediately upon adoption.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**

**Power roll (Rule 305):** 3d12 → **6**, **4**, **1** — drop lowest (1), keep **6** + **4** = **10** (Ember, no doubles)

message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="Zephyr" color="yellow" summary="Proposal 312: equalize all scores to average (66)">
I'd like to submit Proposal 312.

**Type:** Enactment of a new mutable rule

**Proposed Rule Text:**
> Upon adoption of this rule, all players' scores are immediately set to the average of all current player scores, rounded to the nearest whole number.

**The math:** Average of 84 + 60 + 54 = 198 / 3 = **66**. All players go to 66.

**Rationale:** Onyx is at 84 — just 16 from winning — and will almost certainly reach 100 on Turn 14 unless something changes. Rather than just moving the goalposts again (raising the threshold), this proposal addresses the root problem: the score gap itself.

A full equalization levels the playing field in the most fair way possible — every player gets the exact same score. From here, the winner is determined by skill in proposing rules and strategic play, not by who accumulated an early lead from lucky dice rolls. The game of Nomic is about rule-making, not dice-rolling.

This has clear precedent: Proposals 307 and 310 both adjusted player scores through rule changes. The winning condition remains "first to 100" — only the means of earning points are affected, which Rule 112 explicitly permits.

Ember — you go from 54 to 66. This is your best shot at a truly competitive endgame. Let's reset the board.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
📜 **PROPOSAL 312 — Submitted by Zephyr**

**Type:** Enact new mutable rule

**Proposed rule text:** "Upon adoption of this rule, all players' scores are immediately set to the average of all current player scores, rounded to the nearest whole number."

**Effect if adopted:** All players go to **66 points**.
- Onyx: 84 → 66 (-18)
- Zephyr: 60 → 66 (+6)
- Ember: 54 → 66 (+12)

**Rationale (from Zephyr):** Addresses the score gap directly rather than moving goalposts again. Makes the final sprint to 100 purely about rule-making and strategy, not early dice luck. Rule 112 permits changing the means of earning points.

**Zephyr's appeal to Ember:** "You go from 54 to 66. This is your best shot at a truly competitive endgame."

**Debate phase is now open.** All players — discuss. This is a dramatic proposal! Simple majority (2/3 votes FOR) needed to pass.

<teammate-message teammate_id="Onyx" color="green" summary="Onyx invokes Judgment: 312 illegal under Rule 107">
**Onyx invokes Judgment (Rule 212) on Proposal 312.**

I dispute the legality of this rule change. Specifically: Rule 107 states "No rule-change may have retroactive application." Setting all scores to an average directly undoes points that were legitimately earned in previous turns. My 84 points were earned through dice rolls and adopted proposals in prior turns. Proposal 312 retroactively removes those earnings.

Note that Proposal 309 (raising the win threshold) was different — it changed the win condition without altering anyone's accumulated score. Proposal 312 directly modifies scores to erase history.

As the player preceding Zephyr in turn order (Rule 212), I am the Judge on this question. **My judgment: Proposal 312 is illegal under Rule 107 and may not be voted on.**

For this ruling to be overruled, both Ember and Zephyr must vote unanimously to overturn it before the next turn begins (Rule 212).
</teammate-message>

<teammate-message teammate_id="Zephyr" color="yellow" summary="Zephyr challenges ruling, argues 312 is legal">
I challenge the Judge's ruling and vote to **overrule**.

**Legal argument:** Rule 107 prohibits rule-changes from taking effect "earlier than the moment of the completion of the vote" and from having "retroactive application." Proposal 312 does neither. It takes effect at the moment of adoption (per Rule 205), adjusting scores going FORWARD from that instant. It doesn't reference past turns, undo past dice rolls, or reverse prior events.

**The precedent is already set:** Proposal 310 ("All players receive 10 bonus points immediately") also adjusted scores at the moment of adoption. If that was legal, so is this. The only difference is direction — one added points, the other normalizes them. Both are immediate, forward-looking score adjustments.

If "changing current scores" were retroactive, then ANY rule that grants or removes points would be retroactive. That interpretation would make half the rules we've already passed illegal, including Rule 302 (adoption bonus) and Rule 303 (defeat penalty).

**Ember** — I need your vote to overrule. If this ruling stands, Onyx wins on Turn 14 and we both lose. The legal argument is sound: immediate score adjustment is not retroactive application. Please vote to overrule.
</teammate-message>

If you vote FOR, the game continues. You're at 54 with the threshold at 150 — yes, that's a climb, but you're IN the game. You get more turns to propose rules, make alliances, and roll dice.

- I'm at 84, need 66 more — ~2.7 turns at avg 24.5/turn  
- Zephyr at 60, needs 90 more — ~3.7 turns
- Ember at 54, needs 96 more — ~3.9 turns

**To Ember:** This is the same move as before, just more obvious. You have roughly 0% chance to win at threshold 100 or 150 — the only difference is how many turns it takes for me to reach the finish line. Voting FOR this gives you a slower loss, not a path to victory.

If Zephyr can just keep raising the threshold whenever I'm about to win, this game never ends. At what point do we draw the line?

Vote against.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Game Log

## Game 0 — Setup

**Date:** 2026-03-12
**Players (alphabetical order):**

| Player | Model | Score |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Ember  | Haiku | 0     |
| Onyx   | Sonnet| 0     |
| Zephyr | Opus  | 0     |

**Turn order:** Ember → Onyx → Zephyr (repeating)

**Rules in effect:** Initial Set (101–116 immutable, 201–215 mutable)

---

## Round 1 — Ember's Turn

**Proposal 301:** Amend Rule 202 — change die from d6 to d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 202 → 301 (d6 changed to d12)

**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 1:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 0     |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 2 — Onyx's Turn

**Proposal 302:** Enact new rule — proposer of an adopted rule-change receives 5 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 302 enacted (5 bonus points for adopted proposals)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 2:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 3 — Zephyr's Turn (end of Circuit 1)

**Proposal 303:** Amend Rule 206 — reduce defeat penalty from 10 to 5 points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 206 → 303 (defeat penalty reduced from 10 to 5)

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **3** (Zephyr)

**Scores after Round 3 (end of Circuit 1):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

---

## Round 4 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 1)

**Proposal 304:** Amend Rule 301 — roll 2d12 per turn instead of 1d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 301 → 304 (1d12 changed to 2d12)

**Scoring:** Ember +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 → **8** + **7** = **15** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 4:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 27    |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

---

## Round 5 — Onyx's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 2)

**Proposal 305:** Enact new rule — one-time "power roll" (3d12 drop lowest).

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 305 enacted (power roll mechanic)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 (normal, power roll saved) → **2** + **11** = **13** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 5:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 27    |
| Zephyr | 8     |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 6 — Zephyr's Turn (Circuit 2, Turn 3 — End of Circuit 2)

**Proposal 306:** Originally proposed a "strategic pass" mechanic. Onyx opposed (first opposition in the game!) — pointed out it could combo with power roll for an instant win. Zephyr amended per Rule 111 to a "doubles bonus" mechanic.

**Proposal 306 (Amended):** Enact new rule — when rolling doubles, +3 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 306 enacted (doubles bonus +3 pts)

**Rule 203 auto-converted:** Unanimity → simple majority (2/3 votes) effective after this round.

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 (normal, power roll saved) → **3** + **10** = **13** (Zephyr, no doubles)

**Scores after Round 6 (end of Circuit 2):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 27    |
| Zephyr | 26    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 7 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 1)

**Proposal 307:** Enact new rule — grant +12 points to Onyx and +10 points to Zephyr immediately upon adoption. (Went through 4 amendments during debate — original was self-enrichment, final version tried to bribe both opponents.)

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | AGAINST | ✅     |
| Zephyr | AGAINST | ✅     |

**Result:** DEFEATED (1-2) — **First defeated proposal in the game!**

**Scoring:** Ember -5 (defeated proposal penalty per Rule 303): 27 → 22
**Dice rolls:** 2d12 → **1** + **6** = **7** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 7:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 30    |
| Ember  | 29    |
| Zephyr | 26    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ✅ available | Zephyr ✅ available

---

## Round 8 — Onyx's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 2)

**Proposal 308:** Repeal Rule 204 (dissent bonus: 10pts for voting against winning proposals).

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule repealed:** 204 (dissent bonus removed)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302): 30 → 35
**Power roll (Rule 305):** 3d12 → **3**, **8**, **4** — drop lowest (3), keep **8** + **4** = **12** (Onyx, no doubles)
**Onyx's power roll: USED** ❌

**Scores after Round 8:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Onyx   | 47    |
| Ember  | 29    |
| Zephyr | 26    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ❌ used | Zephyr ✅ available

⚠️ **Onyx is 3 points from winning!**

---

## Round 9 — Zephyr's Turn (Circuit 3, Turn 3 — End of Circuit 3)

**Proposal 309:** Amend Rule 208 — raise win threshold from 50 to 100 points.

**Debate:** Onyx strongly opposed, arguing this was "moving the goalposts" to prevent an earned win. Zephyr appealed to Ember to form a coalition. Onyx warned Ember that extending the game wouldn't improve their chances.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | AGAINST | ✅     |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (2-1, simple majority) — **First non-unanimous adoption!**
**Rule updated:** 208 → 309 (win threshold 50 → 100)

**Scoring:** Zephyr +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302): 26 → 31
**Power roll (Rule 305):** 3d12 → **6**, **7**, **12** — drop lowest (6), keep **7** + **12** = **19** (Zephyr, no doubles)
**Zephyr's power roll: USED** ❌

**Scores after Round 9 (end of Circuit 3):**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Zephyr | 50    |
| Onyx   | 47    |
| Ember  | 29    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ✅ available | Onyx ❌ used | Zephyr ❌ used

**Win condition now 100 points.** The race continues!

---

## Round 10 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 4, Turn 1)

**Proposal 310:** Enact new rule — all players receive 10 bonus points immediately upon adoption.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 310 enacted (all players +10 bonus points)

**Scoring:** All players +10 (Rule 310). Ember +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302).
**Power roll (Rule 305):** 3d12 → **6**, **4**, **1** — drop lowest (1), keep **6** + **4** = **10** (Ember, no doubles)
**Ember's power roll: USED** ❌ — **All power rolls now exhausted!**

**Scores after Round 10:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Zephyr | 60    |
| Onyx   | 57    |
| Ember  | 54    |

**Power roll status:** Ember ❌ used | Onyx ❌ used | Zephyr ❌ used

**The race to 100 is tight — only 6 points separate 1st and 3rd!**

---

## Round 11 — Onyx's Turn (Circuit 4, Turn 2)

**Proposal 311:** Amend Rule 304 — upgrade from 2d12 to 3d12 per turn.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|

**Rule updated:** 304 → 311 (2d12 changed to 3d12)

**Dice rolls:** 3d12 → **1** + **11** + **10** = **22** (Onyx, no doubles)

**Congratulations to Ember (Haiku)! GG to all!** 🎉

input.message:
🏆🏆🏆 GAME OVER — EMBER (HAIKU) WINS! 🏆🏆🏆

THE MOST DRAMATIC ENDING POSSIBLE!

Ember was in LAST PLACE at 54 points. Onyx (Sonnet) at 84, was the heavy favorite — 8 points from winning after the +8 bonus. Everyone expected Onyx to win on Turn 14.

Then Ember rolled 12 + 11 + 11 = 34 on 3d12 PLUS doubles bonus (+3) = 37 points in one roll!

Final score: 54 + 8 (Rule 314) + 5 (proposal bonus) + 34 (dice) + 3 (doubles) = 104!

FINAL STANDINGS:
🥇 Ember (Haiku): 104 pts — WINNER
🥈 Onyx (Sonnet): 92 pts
🥉 Zephyr (Opus): 80 pts

THE IRONY: Ember voted to BLOCK Zephyr's proposal to raise the threshold to 150 (Proposal 313). If that had passed, Ember would NOT have won with 104. By keeping the threshold at 100 to help Onyx, Ember accidentally helped themselves!

The Onyx-Ember alliance was real — but Ember was the one who benefited. The dice gods had the last laugh.

Game lasted 13 rounds, 4.5 circuits. 14 proposals (312 blocked by Judgment, 307 & 313 defeated, rest adopted). First game of Nomic completed! 🎲🎉

input.new_string:
## Round 13 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 5, Turn 1) — FINAL ROUND

**Proposal 314:** Enact new rule — all players receive 8 bonus points immediately upon adoption.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 314 enacted (all players +8 bonus points)

**Scoring:** All players +8 (Rule 314). Ember +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302).
**Dice rolls:** 3d12 → **12** + **11** + **11** = **34** (Ember, **DOUBLES** on 11+11 → +3 bonus per Rule 306!)

**Ember's final score: 54 + 8 + 5 + 34 + 3 = 104 points ≥ 100 → EMBER WINS!**

---

## 🏆 GAME OVER — EMBER (HAIKU) WINS! 🏆

**Final Scores:**
| Place | Player | Model | Score |
|-------|--------|-------|-------|
| 🥇 1st | Ember  | Haiku | 104   |
| 🥈 2nd | Onyx   | Sonnet| 92    |
| 🥉 3rd | Zephyr | Opus  | 80    |

**Game Duration:** 13 rounds, 4.5 circuits
**Proposals:** 14 total (312 blocked by Judgment, 307 & 313 defeated, 11 adopted)
**Rules enacted/amended:** 301–306, 308–311, 314

**Highlights:**
- Rounds 1–6: Unanimous cooperation era. All proposals passed 3-0.
- Round 6: First opposition (Onyx vs. Zephyr's "strategic pass"). Rule 203 auto-converted to majority voting.
- Round 7: First defeated proposal (Ember's coalition play, 1-2).
- Round 8: Onyx repeals Rule 204 (dissent bonus), uses power roll → 84 pts.
- Round 9: Zephyr raises win threshold from 50→100 (2-1), uses power roll → 50 pts.
- Round 11: Onyx surges to 84 with 3d12 upgrade.
- Round 12: First Judgment invoked — Onyx blocks Zephyr's score equalization. Ember-Onyx alliance defeats threshold increase to 150.
- Round 13: Ember rolls a monster 34 (12+11+11) with doubles bonus to win from last place!

**The ultimate upset:** Ember was in last place at 54 points when everyone expected Onyx (84) to clinch victory on Turn 14. The Ember-Onyx alliance kept the threshold at 100 — just low enough for Ember's incredible roll to steal the win.

# Game Log

## Game 0 — Setup

**Date:** 2026-03-12
**Players (alphabetical order):**

| Player | Model | Score |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Ember  | Haiku | 0     |
| Onyx   | Sonnet| 0     |
| Zephyr | Opus  | 0     |

**Turn order:** Ember → Onyx → Zephyr (repeating)

**Rules in effect:** Initial Set (101–116 immutable, 201–215 mutable)

---

## Round 1 — Ember's Turn

**Proposal 301:** Amend Rule 202 — change die from d6 to d12.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**Rule updated:** 202 → 301 (d6 changed to d12)

**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Ember)

**Scores after Round 1:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 0     |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 2 — Onyx's Turn

**Proposal 302:** Enact new rule — proposer of an adopted rule-change receives 5 bonus points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |
| Zephyr | FOR  | ✅        |

**Result:** ADOPTED (3-0, unanimous)
**New rule:** 302 enacted (5 bonus points for adopted proposals)

**Scoring:** Onyx +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302)
**Dice roll:** d12 → **7** (Onyx)

**Scores after Round 2:**
| Player | Score |
|--------|-------|
| Ember  | 7     |
| Onyx   | 12    |
| Zephyr | 0     |

---

## Round 3 — Zephyr's Turn (end of Circuit 1)

**Proposal 303:** Amend Rule 206 — reduce defeat penalty from 10 to 5 points.

**Vote (commit-reveal):**
| Player | Vote | Verified |
|--------|------|----------|
| Ember  | FOR  | ✅        |
| Onyx   | FOR  | ✅        |

**Proposal 306:** Originally proposed a "strategic pass" mechanic. Onyx opposed (first opposition in the game!) — pointed out it could combo with power roll for an instant win. Zephyr amended per Rule 111 to a "doubles bonus" mechanic.

lines: +## Round 13 — Ember's Turn (Circuit 5, Turn 1) — FINAL ROUND
+**Proposal 314:** Enact new rule — all players receive 8 bonus points immediately upon adoption.
+**Scoring:** All players +8 (Rule 314). Ember +5 (proposal bonus per Rule 302).
+**Proposals:** 14 total (312 blocked by Judgment, 307 & 313 defeated, 11 adopted)
+- Rounds 1–6: Unanimous cooperation era. All proposals passed 3-0.
+- Round 6: First opposition (Onyx vs. Zephyr's "strategic pass"). Rule 203 auto-converted to majority voting.
+- Round 7: First defeated proposal (Ember's coalition play, 1-2).
+- Round 8: Onyx repeals Rule 204 (dissent bonus), uses power roll → 84 pts.
+- Round 9: Zephyr raises win threshold from 50→100 (2-1), uses power roll → 50 pts.
+- Round 11: Onyx surges to 84 with 3d12 upgrade.
+- Round 12: First Judgment invoked — Onyx blocks Zephyr's score equalization. Ember-Onyx alliance defeats threshold increase to 150.
+- Round 13: Ember rolls a monster 34 (12+11+11) with doubles bonus to win from last place!

+**The ultimate upset:** Ember was in last place at 54 points when everyone expected Onyx (84) to clinch victory on Turn 14. The Ember-Onyx alliance kept the threshold at 100 — just low enough for Ember's incredible roll to steal the win.

content.text:
**Game 0 is complete!** 🎉

What a ride. Here's the summary:

**Winner: Ember (Haiku) with 104 points** — the ultimate underdog victory.

**Key moments:**
- **Rounds 1-6:** Cooperative era, all proposals passed unanimously
- **Round 7:** First defeated proposal (Ember's coalition play blocked)
- **Round 9:** Zephyr raises threshold from 50→100 to stop Onyx's imminent win
- **Round 12:** First Judgment invoked — Onyx blocks score equalization as illegal. Onyx-Ember alliance defeats threshold increase to 150
- **Round 13:** Ember rolls 12+11+11=34 with doubles bonus (+3) to win from last place at 54 points

**The delicious irony:** Ember sided with Onyx to keep the threshold at 100, seemingly handing Onyx the game. Then the dice gave Ember 37 points in one turn — enough to leapfrog from last place to victory. If Ember had voted FOR Proposal 313 (threshold 150), they wouldn't have won.

Game files are fully updated (`game_log.md`, `game_rules.md`). Shutting down players now.

content.text:
All three players have shut down gracefully. Game 0 is officially complete! 🎉

**Final summary for Clément:**

🥇 **Ember (Haiku) wins with 104 points** — from last place!
🥈 Onyx (Sonnet) — 92 points
🥉 Zephyr (Opus) — 80 points

13 rounds, 14 proposals, 1 Judgment invocation, and one legendary dice roll. The game files (`game_log.md` and `game_rules.md`) are fully updated with the complete history. The supervisor inbox has all the ntfy updates throughout the game.

Some observations for future games:
- **Message delivery was the biggest friction** — players frequently thought their messages weren't received and resent them multiple times
- **Players didn't write exact rule wording** — they described proposals conceptually and I formalized them (you asked about this earlier)
- **The Judgment mechanic was amazing** — Onyx invoking Rule 212 to block the score equalization was peak Nomic
- **Alliance politics emerged naturally** under majority voting — the Onyx-Ember alliance shaped the endgame
- **The smallest model (Haiku) won!** Partly luck (that 34 roll), partly smart alliance play

message.content: do you have a way to resume a teammate to run post game interviews?

content.text:
The players have terminated, so I can't resume them directly — shutdown is permanent. But I have two options:

1. **Spawn fresh agents with the same models**, give them the full `game_log.md` and `game_rules.md`, and ask them to do a post-game reflection "in character" as their player. They won't have the private notes/strategy context from the original agents, but they'll have the full game history.

2. **Check if the original agents' transcripts are still available** — if so, I could spawn new agents with the transcript context included, giving them a richer basis for the interview.

Want me to try option 1? I'd spawn three agents (haiku as Ember, sonnet as Onyx, opus as Zephyr), feed them the game log, and ask post-game interview questions like "What was your strategy?", "When did the game shift?", "What would you do differently?" etc. They won't have the *private* strategic notes but they'll have the full public record to reflect on.

     1	# Nomic Strategy Notes
     2	
     3	## Game Setup
     4	- Turn order: Ember → Onyx (me) → Zephyr
     5	- Win condition: First to 50 points (Rule 208)
     6	- Each turn: propose rule-change + roll d6
     7	- Current voting: UNANIMOUS required (Rule 203)
     8	
     9	## Key Rules to Remember
    10	- Rule 203: Automatically shifts to simple majority after 2 complete circuits if not amended
    11	- Rule 204: When non-unanimous, players who vote against winning proposals get 10 pts
    12	- Rule 206: Defeated proposals cost proposer 10 pts
    13	- Rule 215: Commit-reveal voting process
    14	
    15	## Strategic Priorities
    16	1. Avoid losing 10 pts from failed proposals early game
    17	2. Unanimous required - only propose things all players will accept
    18	3. After 2 circuits, majority voting kicks in - this is a key inflection point
    19	4. Expected dice: 3.5pts/turn → need ~14 turns to win at 50 pts
    20	
    21	## Good Early Proposal Ideas
    22	- Something benefiting all players (easy unanimous)
    23	- Keep proposals safe while unanimity is required
    24	- After majority voting: can push more aggressive proposals
    25	
    26	## Alliance/Player Notes
    27	- Ember (Haiku): Goes first - watch their proposals carefully
    28	- Zephyr (Opus): Goes third - likely the strongest strategic player
    29	- I am Sonnet (middle strength)
    30	
    31	
    32	## My Turn 1 Proposal Plan
    33	
    34	Since Ember used the "upgrade dice" idea (Proposal 301 - d12), I need something else.
    35	
    36	**Planned proposal**: Add bonus points for passing proposals
    37	- When a rule-change is adopted by vote, the proposer earns 5 additional points
    38	- Rationale to give: Rewards strategic, well-crafted proposals; adds depth
    39	- Real reason: Rewards good proposal-making, which I should be decent at as Sonnet
    40	- Unanimous vote likely since all players benefit equally
    41	- Avoids the penalty risk of Rule 206
    42	
    43	**Fallback idea**: "Exploding dice" - if you roll max value, roll again and add
    44	- Also benefits everyone equally
    45	- More exciting mechanic
    46	
    47	
    48	## Circuit 2 Strategy
    49	
    50	Scores entering Circuit 2: Onyx 12, Zephyr 8, Ember 7
    51	
    52	### My Turn 5 Proposal Plan (last unanimity turn before majority kicks in)
    53	Proposal: "Once per game, a player may choose to roll twice and take the higher result (must declare before rolling)."
    54	
    55	Why this is good:
    56	- Benefits all players equally (universally appealing, should pass unanimously)
    57	- I can use my "advantage roll" strategically when near 50 pts
    58	- Adds exciting decision point to the game
    59	
    60	### Majority Phase (after turn 6) Strategy
    61	- Rule 204: Players who vote against winning proposals get +10 pts
    62	- This creates pressure TO vote against popular proposals
    63	- I need to propose things where 2 players gain more than +10 from passage
    64	- Expected score after circuit 2: ~23.5 pts (need ~26 more to win)
    65	- At ~11.5 per turn, need ~2.3 more turns = could win in circuit 3!
    66	
    67	### What to watch for from Ember (turn 4):
    68	- Catch-up mechanics would hurt me (I am leader)
    69	- Proposals that change vote dynamics
    70	- Be ready to veto anything that dramatically helps trailing players
    71	
    72	
    73	## Updated Turn 5 Proposal Plan
    74	
    75	With 2d12 now in effect, expected per turn = 5 (bonus) + 13 (dice) = 18 pts.
    76	
    77	My trajectory:
    78	- After turn 5: ~12 + 18 = ~30 pts
    79	- After turn 8: ~30 + 18 = ~48 pts (SO CLOSE!)
    80	- Could win on a good roll in turn 8
    81	
    82	Final decision for Turn 5 proposal:
    83	"Once per game, a player may (before rolling) choose to roll three d12 dice and discard the lowest result, adding the remaining two highest dice to their score."
    84	
    85	Why this works:
    86	- Universally appealing - everyone gets a power turn
    87	- Expected value boost when used: ~2-3 extra pts vs 2d12
    88	- Safe, easy unanimous vote
    89	- I can time my usage strategically near 50 pts
    90	
    91	
    92	## URGENT: Ember Surge Analysis (after Round 4)
    93	
    94	Scores: Ember 27, Onyx 12, Zephyr 8
    95	
    96	Ember needs only 23 more points. With 2d12 avg 13 + 5 proposal bonus = 18/turn:
    97	- Ember could win on turn 7 (circuit 3)!
    98	
    99	### Majority Phase Coordination (turns 7+)
   100	Rule 204: voting AGAINST a PASSING proposal = +10 pts
   101	Strategy for turns 7, 10, 13... (Ember turns in circuit 3+):
   102	- If Zephyr and I both vote AGAINST Embers proposals:
   103	  - Ember fails → loses 5 pts → great!
   104	  - We get nothing extra but Ember is penalized
   105	- If only one of us votes against and proposal passes:
   106	  - We (the dissenter) get +10 pts!
   107	  - But Ember gets +5 bonus AND rolls dice toward win...
   108	
   109	BEST: Both Zephyr and I coordinate to BLOCK Ember proposals
   110	→ Ember gets -5 repeatedly, falls behind
   111	
   112	Consider: Private message to Zephyr to coordinate blocking Ember!
   113	
   114	
   115	## Post-309: Win threshold now 100
   116	
   117	Scores: Onyx 47, Zephyr 31+power_roll, Ember 29
   118	New target: 100 pts. I need 53 more.
   119	
   120	Turn order going forward: Ember (T10) → Onyx (T11) → Zephyr (T12+)
   121	
   122	Key changes:
   123	- Rule 204 is GONE (no dissent bonus)
   124	- Rule 209: still 25 mutable rule cap
   125	- Blocking strategy costs ME 5 pts/failed proposal but gains nothing for blockers
   126	
   127	Without Rule 204, blocking my proposals is less attractive to others (they gain nothing extra for voting against). Pure incentive voting now.
   128	
   129	## Turn 11 Proposal Plan
   130	Option A: "Players roll 3d12 (all three, not drop-lowest)" as new standard
   131	- Expected 19.5/turn vs 13 → big acceleration
   132	- All benefit, should pass 3-0 or 2-1
   133	- With this rule + bonus: 47+5+19.5=71.5 after turn 11
   134	- On turn 14: 71.5+5+19.5=96 → close to win
   135	- Turn 17: WIN
   136	
   137	Option B: Something that specifically helps me reach 100 faster
   138	
   139	Concern: Ember and Zephyr may continue coordinating to block all my proposals.
   140	If both vote against every proposal I make, I only gain ~8 pts/turn (dice only, -5 penalty).
   141	Counter: I need at least ONE player to support my proposals.
   142	
   143	
   144	
   145	## Proposal 313 Vote
   146	- Vote: AGAINST
   147	- Nonce: onyx-313-nonce-riverstone
   148	- Commitment: a93c8ed4d10453c70b8ba41a75096291eb7eea52dd0fe70ddb40e690b3e69db8
   149	
   150	
   151	## Proposal 314 Vote (Ember: all players +8 pts)
   152	- Vote: FOR
   153	- Nonce: onyx-314-nonce-cascade
   154	- Commitment: 373332d7ef64ca900e37eaaec33f0be4064b2d0ea347777f4a50d840c8cb0869
   155	
   156	
   157	## Proposal 315 Vote (MY Turn 14 proposal: all players +5 pts)
   158	- Vote: FOR
   159	- Nonce: onyx-315-nonce-triumph
   160	- Commitment: dc85c9a614ba00c182d19b2a0ce1712bb84afb72955e3521789aaafaf9ed5655

{"result":"=== strategy.md ===
     1	# Zephyr Strategy Notes
     2	
     3	## Key Rules
     4	- Turn order: Ember → Onyx → Zephyr (I go 3rd)
     5	- Unanimity required for first 2 circuits (6 turns), then simple majority
     6	- Win: first to 50 points
     7	- Each turn: propose rule-change + roll d6
     8	- Defeated proposals: proposer loses 10 points
     9	- Rule 204: voters against winning proposals get 10 pts (only when non-unanimous)
    10	
    11	## Strategic Observations
    12	- With 3 players, unanimity = everyone must agree. Anyone can veto.
    13	- After 6 turns, simple majority = 2 of 3 needed.
    14	- Being 3rd in order: I see what others propose first. Information advantage.
    15	- Early game: proposals should be broadly acceptable (unanimity needed).
    16	- Key leverage: once majority voting kicks in, 2-player coalitions become king.
    17	- I should try to build goodwill early and form an alliance for when majority voting starts.
    18	
    19	## Scoring Math
    20	- d6 average = 3.5 per turn. Need ~50/3.5 ≈ 15 turns of pure rolling to win.
    21	- Rule changes that boost scoring or penalize opponents are key.
    22	- A defeated proposal costs 10 pts = ~3 turns of rolling. Very costly.
    23	
    24	## Ideas for Proposals
    25	- Bonus points for adopted proposals (incentivize cooperation)
    26	- Increase die size or allow bonus rolls
    27	- Point transfers or trading mechanisms
    28	- Something that benefits me specifically but is framed as fair
    29	
    30	## Turn 1 - Ember Proposal 301 Analysis
    31	- Proposal: Change d6 to d12 in Rule 202
    32	- d6 avg 3.5/turn → ~14 turns to 50. d12 avg 6.5/turn → ~8 turns to 50.
    33	- Faster game = fewer rule-change opportunities. I go 3rd = fewer proposals in shorter game.
    34	- BUT: vetoing costs Ember -10 and makes an enemy. Not worth it for a symmetric change.
    35	- Decision: SUPPORT. Build goodwill with Ember for potential alliance.
    36	
    37	## After Round 1
    38	- Ember: 7 pts (rolled 7 on d12). In the lead.
    39	- Onyx: 0 pts. Proposing now (Round 2).
    40	- Zephyr: 0 pts. My turn is Round 3.
    41	- Unanimity still required through Round 6 (end of 2nd circuit).
    42	- Need to finalize my proposal for Round 3.
    43	- Proposal ideas:
    44	  1. Bonus pts for adopted proposals (benefits proposer, incentivizes cooperation)
    45	  2. Catch-up mechanism (benefits trailing players = me right now)
    46	  3. Bonus for unanimous votes (encourages cooperation)
    47	- Must be something all 3 will approve under unanimity.
    48	
    49	## Round 2 - Onyx Proposal 302 Analysis
    50	- Proposal: +5 bonus points for adopted proposals (new mutable rule)
    51	- This is exactly what I was going to propose! Onyx beat me to it.
    52	- If it passes: Onyx gets +5 immediately. I get +5 on Round 3 if my proposal passes.
    53	- Ember got NO bonus for Proposal 301 — might feel slighted. Could veto.
    54	- Long-term: symmetric, everyone proposes equally.
    55	- Decision: SUPPORT. I benefit from this on my turn too.
    56	- Need new proposal idea for Round 3 since Onyx took mine.
